name: Trade Ingestion + Validation
summary: Drop invalid rows, type-cast trade dates, regex-extract a commodity code, filter to current-year activity, and pivot a per-book exposure view from the nightly booking-system extract.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Nightly booking-system extract: WTI, Brent, Henry Hub, TTF, JKM, PJM trades.
# Schema: trade_id, trader_id, trade_date, value_date, settlement_date,
#         counterparty_id, instrument, commodity, product_type, notional, unit,
#         price, currency, buy_sell, book, portfolio, region, delivery_location, booked_at
trades = pd.read_csv("trades.csv")

# Booking systems sometimes emit half-written rows on overnight cutover.
trades = trades.dropna(subset=["trade_id", "trade_date", "notional"])

# Normalise the two date columns Risk uses for valuation.
trades["trade_date"] = pd.to_datetime(trades["trade_date"]).dt.date
trades["value_date"] = pd.to_datetime(trades["value_date"]).dt.date

# Pull the commodity prefix off the instrument string (e.g. WTI-DEC25 -> WTI).
trades["commodity_code"] = trades["instrument"].str.extract(r"^([A-Z]+)")

# Drop non-positive notionals and any future-dated rows.
filtered = trades[(trades["notional"] > 0) & (trades["trade_date"] <= "2026-04-26")]

# Per-book daily exposure across CRUDE / NATGAS / POWER.
exposure = filtered.pivot(index="book", columns="commodity", values="notional")
exposure.to_csv("trade_blotter.csv")

name: Trade Dedup Across Booking Systems
summary: Stack overlapping Endur, Allegro, and internal extracts during a migration window, dedup on (trade_id, version), keep the most recent booked copy, and emit a consolidated trade tape.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Three parallel booking systems during the Endur -> Allegro migration window.
# All three emit the canonical trade schema plus a `version` increment.
endur = pd.read_csv("endur_trades.csv")
allegro = pd.read_csv("allegro_trades.csv")
internal = pd.read_csv("internal_trades.csv")

# Stack the three extracts into a single tape.
combined = pd.concat([endur, allegro, internal])

# Dedup on (trade_id, version) so a version-stamped amendment is kept once.
combined = combined.drop_duplicates(subset=["trade_id", "version"])

# Most-recently booked copy wins any reporting tie.
combined = combined.sort_values("booked_at", ascending=False)

combined.to_csv("consolidated_trades.csv", index=False)

name: Trade Amend Tracking
summary: Join the booking-system amend log to the canonical trade record, then filter to material amendments where the new notional moved more than 0.5% from the original — the audit slice middle-office reviews each morning.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Original trades extracted EOD; amends.csv from the booking-system audit log.
# trades.csv columns: trade_id, trader_id, trade_date, counterparty_id,
#   instrument, commodity, notional, price, currency, book, region.
# amends.csv columns: amend_id, trade_id, amended_at, field_changed,
#   old_notional, new_notional, old_price, new_price, amended_by.
trades = pd.read_csv("trades.csv")
amends = pd.read_csv("amends.csv")

# Inner-join amends to the canonical trade record by trade_id.
joined = amends.merge(trades, on="trade_id", how="inner")

# Material amend: notional changed by more than 0.5% from the booked value.
audit = joined[
    (joined["new_notional"] - joined["old_notional"]).abs()
    > (joined["old_notional"] * 0.005)
]

audit.to_csv("amend_audit.csv", index=False)

name: Trade Fail Reconciliation
summary: Roll up the post-trade failure queue by (counterparty, reason_code), then sort the worst counterparties to the top so middle-office knows who to chase.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Daily failed-trade extract from the post-trade settlement queue.
# trade_fails.csv: fail_id, trade_id, counterparty_id, settlement_date,
#   reason_code (UNMATCHED / DK / INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS / WRONG_CURRENCY /
#   COUNTERPARTY_LIMIT), notional, currency, age_days
fails = pd.read_csv("trade_fails.csv")

# Roll up by (counterparty, reason) so middle office can chase the worst.
by_cp_reason = fails.groupby(["counterparty_id", "reason_code"]).agg({
    "fail_id": "count",
    "notional": "sum",
}).reset_index()

# Sort the top offenders to the top of the queue.
ranked = by_cp_reason.sort_values("fail_id", ascending=False)
ranked.to_csv("fails_by_counterparty.csv", index=False)

name: Broker-Confirm STP Matching
summary: STP-match internal trades against broker confirms by stacking both feeds and dropping duplicates on (trade_id, execution_time) — the canonical OTC commodity confirmation pattern.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Internal trades vs broker confirms — STP matching for OTC commodity trades.
# Both sides share trade_id + execution_time on a successful match.
internal_trades = pd.read_csv("internal_trades.csv")
broker_confirms = pd.read_csv("broker_confirms.csv")

# Stack into one tape of candidate matches.
combined = pd.concat([internal_trades, broker_confirms])

# A confirm-match key: drop duplicates on (trade_id, execution_time) so
# the matched pair collapses to a single row in the consolidated tape.
matched = combined.drop_duplicates(subset=["trade_id", "execution_time"])

matched.to_csv("broker_confirm_matched.csv", index=False)
